title: put() uses SSA for no-key and merge-patch for ?key=; asymmetry undocumented
description: lib.rs:171-213: put() with no ?key= uses SSA (Patch::Apply, field manager 'secretx-k8s'); put() with ?key= uses JSON merge patch. SSA semantics differ from merge-patch: SSA tracks field ownership and conflicts; merge-patch does not. A user mixing both strategies on the same Secret (e.g., put ?key=a via SSA on a related path) will encounter Kubernetes field manager conflicts. The design rationale is not documented. No comment explains WHY two different patch strategies are used. Future contributors will not understand the invariant and may normalize them incorrectly. — Risk: silent data-loss or 409 conflicts when strategies interact.
